title: 2 Corinthians 4:17
message: For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal weight of glory that far outweighs them all.
notes: Paul calls suffering light and momentary — not because it is painless, but because of what it is producing. The weight of glory being accumulated is infinitely heavier than the weight of any trial I carry now.

title: John 17:3
message: Now this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent.
notes: Eternal life is not a destination I arrive at after death — it is a quality of knowing I step into now. To know the Father and the Son is already to have begun the life that never ends.

title: John 11:25-26
message: Jesus said to her, 'I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die; and whoever lives by believing in me will never die. Do you believe this?'
notes: Jesus does not point to the resurrection — He declares Himself to be it. My hope is not in an event but in a Person. And He turns it personal: 'Do you believe this?' The question is still being asked of me today.

title: Romans 8:11
message: And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies because of his Spirit who lives in you.
notes: Resurrection power is not reserved for the last day — it is currently at work in my mortal body through the Spirit. The same power that emptied the tomb is active in me right now, giving life where death would otherwise take hold.

title: Philippians 3:10-11
message: I want to know Christ — yes, to know the power of his resurrection and participation in his sufferings, becoming like him in his death, and so, somehow, attaining to the resurrection from the dead.
notes: Paul's deepest ambition was to know the power of the resurrection — not just the fact of it. Resurrection power is a present experience, not only a future event. I pursue this same intimacy with the risen Christ.

title: 1 Corinthians 15:54-55
message: When the perishable has been clothed with the imperishable, and the mortal with immortality, then the saying that is written will come true: 'Death has been swallowed up in victory.' 'Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?'
notes: Death gets the last taunt. Paul turns it into a victory song. The sting of death has already been removed by the resurrection — the final clothing in immortality will simply make visible what is already true in Christ.

title: Romans 6:9
message: For we know that since Christ was raised from the dead, he cannot die again; death no longer has mastery over him.
notes: Death's authority over Christ ended permanently at the resurrection. And since I am in Christ, that same authority no longer has the final word over my life. I share His exemption.

title: 2 Corinthians 4:14
message: Because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus from the dead will also raise us with Jesus and present us with you to himself.
notes: The same God who raised Jesus will raise me — it is not a different power applied to a lesser cause. He who raised the greatest will certainly raise the rest. My resurrection is guaranteed by the precedent of Christ's.

title: 1 Peter 1:3-4
message: Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead, and into an inheritance that can never perish, spoil or fade — kept in heaven for you.
notes: A living hope — not a static wish but an active, breathing expectation rooted in the resurrection. And the inheritance behind it cannot perish, spoil, or fade. Nothing I am waiting for in God can be devalued, stolen, or corrupted.

title: Romans 5:2
message: Through whom we have gained access by faith into this grace in which we now stand. And we boast in the hope of the glory of God.
notes: I boast in the hope of glory — not in present circumstances but in the certain destination they are producing. Hope in God is not passive wishing; it is confident expectation that glory is where this is all heading.

title: Colossians 1:27
message: To them God has chosen to make known among the Gentiles the glorious riches of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
notes: Christ in me is the hope of glory — not Christ above me, not Christ ahead of me, but Christ within me right now. My hope for eternal glory is not a distant aspiration; it lives inside me as a present indwelling reality.

title: Titus 2:13
message: While we wait for the blessed hope — the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
notes: The blessed hope — a specific, named hope with a specific content. I am not hoping for vague improvement; I am waiting for a Person. The return of Christ is the anchor point of every eternal hope I carry.

title: John 5:24
message: Very truly I tell you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and will not be judged but has crossed over from death to life.
notes: Has crossed over — past tense. The transfer from death to life already happened the moment I believed. I am not heading toward eternal life; I have already entered it. The crossing is complete.

title: 1 John 5:11-12
message: And this is the testimony: God has given us eternal life, and this life is in his Son. Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
notes: Eternal life is in the Son — and I have the Son. Therefore I have the life. It is not granted at death; it is possessed now in union with Christ. This is testimony, not aspiration.

title: Ephesians 2:7
message: In order that in the coming ages he might show the incomparable riches of his grace, expressed in his kindness to me in Christ Jesus.
notes: The coming ages — my story with God does not end at death or even at the resurrection. There are ages ahead in which God will continue displaying His kindness toward me in Christ. Eternity is not a static state; it is an ongoing revelation of grace.

title: Romans 8:18
message: I consider that our present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed in us.
notes: The glory will be revealed in us — not merely shown to us but manifested through us. Present suffering loses its weight entirely when placed next to the scale of what God intends to reveal through my life in eternity.

title: 1 Corinthians 15:20
message: But Christ has indeed been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
notes: Firstfruits guarantees the full harvest. Christ's resurrection is not a singular miracle — it is the preview and promise of mine. Where He went, I am destined to follow. His rising is the down payment on my own.

title: 2 Timothy 1:10
message: But it has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who has destroyed death and has brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.
notes: Death has been destroyed — not weakened, not postponed. Christ abolished it at the cross and resurrection and brought immortality into the light. I walk in the daylight of a reality death has no authority over.

title: Revelation 1:18
message: I am the Living One; I was dead, and now look, I am alive for ever and ever! And I hold the keys of death and Hades.
notes: The keys of death and Hades are in Christ's hands — not death's hands. He holds the authority over what death can and cannot do. My eternity is not subject to death's jurisdiction; it is subject to His.

title: 2 Corinthians 4:18
message: So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen, since what is seen is temporary, but what is unseen is eternal.
notes: Fixing my eyes is a deliberate, active choice — it requires redirecting my focus away from the visible and toward the eternal. What I can see is the least permanent thing in my life. What I cannot yet see is what will last forever.
